content:
A dialog being open and an order being approved are both “state,” but they should not be owned, stored, or managed in the same way.

6 min read
4 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

State management discussions in Angular often begin with tools.
Should we use Signals?
Should we use NgRx?
Should we use Signal Store?
Should we keep everything inside a service?
These are useful questions, but they come too early.
Before deciding how to store state, we should understand what kind of state we are dealing with.
A modal being open is state.
A selected tab is state.
The currently authenticated user is state.
An approved invoice is also state.
But these values have very different responsibilities, lifetimes, and owners.
Treating all of them the same creates unnecessary complexity.
Not Every State Value Deserves a Store
Consider a Customer page with the following values:
readonly isDialogOpen =…
